How fresh is this? Full rebuild end-of-day after the close on the 2026-07-17 data, then refreshed hourly during US market hours (prices, intraday options-flow signals; the AI reads stay on the EOD batch). Stock & technical screeners update once a day (EOD), options-flow screeners (Intraday ~30 min) refresh during the session. Insider feeds are daily, 13F is quarterly. Each card shows its own refresh cadence and data date. Screeners are ordered highest data confidence first.

★ Cross-screener confluence

CONFLUENCE
🧠 Flags tickers where 2 or more coherent screeners trigger within 1 trading day, score >= 65, with validated combos ranked first by historical edge.
Backtested combos first (May-2026 sweeps + pairs battery), then today's coherent overlaps. Trade-ready funnel: best_trades_today →

Generic confluence carries the bucket-level stats only (3+ VALID screeners: WR 81.7% at +20d). A pair NOT listed above has no proven pair-level edge - treat the overlap as attention, not size. New-screener pairs re-enter the battery as their history grows.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

UNH leads today's batch with a score of 286, firing on 4 screeners (CLENOW_MOMENTUM, PEAD_5D, PULLBACK, VOLUME_LEADER) - a clean gap over MS at 180 (CLENOW_MOMENTUM, PEAD_5D, PULLBACK, VARIANCE_PREMIUM) and MNST at 146, which adds SMART_MONEY_SURGE and VOLUME_LEADER. The score distribution is top-heavy: UNH towers above a tight cluster from 100 to 146 across the remaining seven names in a 20-overlap batch. Critically, no ticker today fires a validated combo - matched_combo is null across all 10 names, meaning none carry the 81.7% win rate or Sharpe 2.68 from the 3+ VALID screener bucket. NEUTRAL macro at 51.7 is neither headwind nor tailwind, which keeps the bar higher on discretionary sizing. Take UNH first at moderate conviction on score dominance alone, sized below a validated-combo entry.

🎈 Blow-off top detector NEW

BLOWOFF_DETECTOR
🧠 Spots parabolic tops about to roll over on the top-sector winners — Greenwood-Shleifer-You 2-year rallies, Edwards-Magee parabolic, gap exhaustion, island reversals and LPPLS variance acceleration. Only the max-drawdown-backtested signals carry weight; the academic heuristics confirm. GO / NO GO on naked puts comes from the live Polygon options chain.

Read
Top blow-off candidate today: PYPL (score 7, target ~$53.81 (DD20d -5.0%)).

1 ticker(s) fire at least ONE Bonferroni-validated backtested signal (25y, 50,109 obs, p<0.001). 0 stack ≥3 points = high conviction.

Methodology: the backtest-validated signals measure max drawdown over 20d (not the mean forward return — masked by rebounds in a bull market). The academic heuristics (Greenwood-Shleifer-You, Edwards-Magee, gap, island, LPPLS) stay displayed for context but do not survive Bonferroni on their own — they only confirm.
How to read this
Backtest-validated signals (25 years, 91 tickers, 50,109 observations, p<0.001):
• Two-red parabolic (weight 3) — 2 red days after parabolic. DD 20d -11.55%, lift +20pp, N=302.
• Stoch cross<80 + parabolic (weight 2) — DD 20d -9.65%, N=148.
• Rally + red on volume (weight 2) — rally 10%+ then red day. DD 20d -7.23%, N=93.
• Shooting star / BB fail / Bearish engulf (weight 1) — DD 20d -3 to -5%.

Options decision = multi-factor composite 0-100 (IV Rank · IV-HV · term struct · skew 25Δ · implied/DD ratio · liquidity). Score ≥70 + liquidity OK → 🟢 GO ATM Put. Score 50-70 → 🟡 GO LIGHT OTM 25Δ. <50 or liquidity KO → 🔴 NO GO + reason.

Global composite REJECT on its own (mean forward return bull-biased). Individual signals VALID on the max-DD metric.

🧠 Detects institutional option sweeps confirmed by next-day OI delta and EMA8/21 alignment, filtering genuine accumulation signals from noise.
Construction
Unusual options sweep + next-day OI delta confirmation (OPENING vs CLOSING) + EMA8/21 structure + low-OI anomaly. Score includes OI Delta, premium $, signal persistence, EMA alignment.
Top picks: $KDP
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayPatternTypeStrikePremiumOI verdictOI ΔPersistEMAStreakNet 20d
1KDP ↑50 Watch$30.53 -1.23%BULLISH SWEEPCALL31$227KOPENING250258STRONG22
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Starts from the prior day's UNUSUAL signals, then checks T+1 OI Delta
  • OI Delta: OPENING (+25), LIKELY_OPENING (+15), CLOSING (-50), NEUTRAL (+5)
  • 20d flow persistence: net≥5 → +30, ≥3 → +20, ≥2 → +10, ≤-2 → -15; streak bonus ≥3
  • Multi-leg spread detected: -30; Low-OI anomaly: +15
  • EMA STRONG +10 / PULLBACK +5 / BROKEN -10; quality HIGH +5 / LOW -5 (min mcap $500M)
  • Archives only non-CLOSING signals AND score ≥ 50
Score
clamp(0,100, OI_delta + persistence + spread + low_oi + ema + quality). CLOSING dropped. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The live tape shows 100% win rate and 200% average 20-day return versus SPY's 3.17%, but the verdict is WATCH given only 2 of 264 signals have resolved - the April through June monthly cohorts each show 100% win rates, yet 2 confirmed exits does not validate the 68.13% backtest win rate.

HALF SIZEinsufficient data (2 resolved, need ≥20)
Last 90 days: 264 signals captured · 2 resolved at +20d · WR 100% · avg +200.0% · vs SPY +3.2%
Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 264 closed · WR TB 100% · 0% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

Today's batch is one name: KDP at a score of 50, the Watch-tier floor, on a bullish sweep. No signal scores 65 or above and none reach 80, so the distribution is as thin as this screener produces. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 51.7 provides no directional tailwind for a minimum-threshold sweep name. 🧠 Ranks stocks where IV30 exceeds HV30, applying Sinclair sector premia and value factors to score premium-selling candidates 0-100.
Construction
IV30 vs HV30 spread + sector bias (Sinclair Table 4.7: Utilities +31%, Tech -8%) + value factors (P/E, RoE, mcap). Score 0-100 ranks premium-selling candidates.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Liquid universe: market cap ≥ $2B, volume ≥ 500K
  • VP spread (IV Rank vs HV30) - weight 35%
  • Sinclair sector bias (Utilities +31% … Tech -8%) - weight 25%
  • Value factors (P/E, RoE, RoA, D/E, mcap) - weight 25%; IV Rank - weight 15%
  • Garbage-stock bonus (high P/E, low RoE) up to +15
Score
clamp(0,100, vp×0.35 + sector×0.25 + value×0.25 + ivr×0.15 + garbage_bonus). LONG (sell puts), top 30.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape confirms the model: 79.8% win rate and +3.79% average over 20 days against SPY at +0.05% beats the index by +3.7 points, and June's cohort at 81.4% WR shows the edge strengthening rather than fading.

🧠 Identifies stocks in the mid-VRP tertile where implied vol sits coherently above realized vol, avoiding catalyst richness and market-disbelief cheapness at the extremes.
Construction
Longs tickers in the MID tertile of the variance risk premium (IV vs realized): options priced coherently with realized vol, neither too cheap (LOW = market disbelief) nor too rich (HIGH = catalyst already priced).

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Reads the variance_premium_daily table on the latest day
  • Keeps only the MID tertile (OOS sweet spot: neither LOW cheap nor HIGH catalyst-priced)
  • Sorted by vrp_proxy ascending, top 30
Score
max(50, 100 − rank×50/n) within the MID tertile. LONG.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

PRGS at 100 and ROL at 99 lead today's batch, both Very strong tier. AMCR at 97 rounds out the top three, and all three carry null pattern fields, removing one confirmation layer. The distribution is top-heavy: 13 of 30 signals score at or above 80 with a median of 75, putting over a third of the batch in high-conviction territory. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 51.7 is a non-factor, neither amplifying nor filtering the list. Start with PRGS at full conviction, ROL as a close second.

🧠 Detects stocks where put volume exceeds 3x the 30-day average in a stable tape, betting institutional hedging in calm conditions precedes upside via IV crush.
Construction
Put volume > 3× avg(30d) + price stable or up. Kerberos thesis: contrarian - institutional hedging activity in calm tape often precedes upside continuation. Score weighs put vol ratio, price stability, IV crush.

#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayPut volPut ratioPut premium

4SANM ↑67● Signal$202.83 +2.83%20593.38$2716K

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • For each ticker on the latest options_daily_flow day
  • Trigger: put_volume ≥ 3.0× the 30d moving average of put_volume
  • AND put_volume ≥ 100 contracts
Score
min(100, put_ratio × 20) where put_ratio = put_volume / 30d average. LONG (contrarian: hedging = bottom near).
🧠 AI read · today's batch

The score distribution is entirely undifferentiated, all 50 signals sitting at the 100 ceiling with a median of 100, meaning the screener is operating as a binary gate today rather than a ranking tool. ADNT, AMSC, and AOSL represent the pick list as well as any others, but all carry an identical score of 100 and tier of Very strong with no pattern data to separate them. The NEUTRAL macro regime (score 51.7) neither supports nor undermines a contrarian hedging thesis, though a regime with directional conviction would give this setup more lift. With zero score dispersion and no distinguishing patterns across 50 signals, first-mover preference is arbitrary - if forced to act, size small across two or three names and treat this batch as a watch list pending a confirming catalyst rather than a trade.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Excluded if pulled back > 25% below the 52-week high
  • Uptrend: 90d slope > +0.20%/day, 60d slope > +0.25%/day, 90d R² > 0.70, price > EMA50 and EMA200
  • Pullback in progress: 30d slope < +0.15%/day, 10d slope < +0.05%/day, RSI(14) between 40 and 65 (sweet spot 45-55)
  • Healthy pullback: 10d/30d volume < 0.80, ATR contracting, no down gaps, Fibonacci level 38/50/62%, <15% below 52w high
  • Recovery: higher lows, rising 5d volume, bullish RSI divergence
Score
Uptrend (max 30) + Pullback (max 25) + Healthy (max 25) + Recovery (max 30). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The live tape directly contradicts the backtest's 65% win rate: 491 resolved trades over 90 days returned a 49.7% WR and avg -1.34% vs SPY +0.31%, a -1.7 pt gap that earned the KILL verdict, and while monthly cohorts improved from Feb's 24.6% WR to Jun's 60.9%, that trend has not yet rehabilitated the live signal.
📊 Recent tracking (last 90 days, ticker by ticker)
STARTER ONLYWR 50% · avg -1.3% vs SPY +0.3% (-1.7 pts)
Last 90 days: 620 signals captured · 491 resolved at +20d · WR 50% · avg -1.3% · vs SPY +0.3%
Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 497 closed · WR TB 41% · 17% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

MANU, MGM, and SXC lead at score 100 with the LAUNCH pattern, and ASML slots in at 95, adding a large-cap semi equipment name with real liquidity to what is otherwise a thin-volume group. Every one of the 50 signals scores 80 or above and the median sits at 90, so the batch is ceiling-compressed with no score gradient to separate candidates. Macro is NEUTRAL at 51.7, which provides no regime tailwind for a reversal strategy that benefits from risk-on conditions. The structural red flag is that in live 90d tracking, Very strong-tier names - exactly where today's top picks sit - post the worst numbers: 38% WR and avg -3.5%, worse than both Strong and Signal tiers. If forced to act, ASML is the single preference on liquidity and fundamental quality, but size should be minimal given what the live tape is showing for this tier.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

LUMN leads at 91 (Very strong tier), the only name at that tier in today's batch, and the Very strong cohort in 90d tracking carries a 74% win rate and +16.4% average, making it the cleanest candidate on the board. TDS at 88 and AEM, ARDX, WIT at 85 fill in the next layer, all Strong tier. The score distribution is workable rather than exceptional: median 78, 13 names at or above 80, all 35 signals clearing 65, no dangerous top-heaviness but no standout concentration of high-conviction signals either. A NEUTRAL macro regime (score 51.7) does not block a contrarian mean-reversion basket but removes any regime tailwind, so entries stay disciplined at basket sizing. Take LUMN first, 1-2% notional via LEAPS, medium conviction until pattern data populates.

🧠 Detects vol-of-vol climax using VVIX percentile and VIX term structure to flag sell-vol opportunities at peak fear.
Construction
VVIX percentile + VIX term structure (VIX3M/VIX) + FOMC proximity + weekend effect. Detects 'vol-of-vol' climax (sell-vol opportunities at the top of fear).
Top picks: $SPY
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayVerdictComposite
1SPY ↑64 Watch$742.97 -0.04%SELL VOL1.62
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Market-level signal archived under SPY (Sinclair composite)
  • VVIX: ≥90th pctile → SELL VOL (+2), ≤10th → BUY VOL (-2)
  • Term structure VIX3M/VIX < 1.0 contango → +1; VIX regime 13-19 sweet +1, >24 danger -1
  • Weekend (Friday) +0.5; FOMC 3d ahead +0.5; Hurst/timing optional
Score
sum of sub-scores (~-6 to +6). ≥3.5 STRONG SELL VOL … ≤-3.5 STRONG BUY VOL. Archived score = clamp(0,100,(total+6)/12×100).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape's 71% WR and +1.4% avg edge SPY by just 0.1 points overall, with the Signal tier doing the heavy lifting at 90% WR and +2.15% avg on 10 trades, while monthly cohort avg returns have compressed sharply from +5.4% in April to +0.57% in June - a deteriorating trend that partially contradicts the VALID verdict and argues for waiting on Signal-tier prints rather than acting on Watch entries.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Per-contract filters: Vol/OI > 3.0×, volume > 500, premium > $100K, contract OI ≥ 10, chain OI ≥ 5000
  • Expiration 7-365 DTE; deep ITM excluded
  • Pattern: delta>0.7 → HEDGE, call → BULLISH SWEEP, put → BEARISH SWEEP
  • Timing: LATE (-15) if chasing a >3% move, EARLY (+10)
  • V3 hard filters (before archive): volume < 1.5× the 20d average, |price-EMA20| < 5%, timing ≠ LATE
Score
Vol/OI (max 30) + log premium (max 30) + OTM (max 20) + delta (max 20) + timing (-15/+10) + IV compression (IVRank<15 +15). Archived if ≥50.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live tape prints a 100% win rate on 15 resolved signals and a 196% average return vs. SPY's 0.22%, but with only 15 of 620 signals resolved the sample is too thin to confirm or contradict the backtest's 61% win rate and borderline p-value of 0.097, and the WATCH verdict stands on insufficient data alone.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

TLT leads at 82 with a HEDGE pattern - institutional defensiveness in a NEUTRAL macro (51.7) rather than directional conviction, so it reads as a risk-off positioning tell, not a trade to front-run. HIMS at 80 is the cleanest name in the batch: Strong tier, clean bullish sweep, no contradictory read. Score distribution is thin above 70 with only 2 names clearing 80 and a median of 57 across 27 signals, confirming this is a selective rather than broad flow day. INDA and CMPS both register bearish sweeps tagged as LONG direction, a mixed read that cuts their signal value until that contradiction resolves. Take HIMS first at high conviction, COIN (76, Signal, bullish sweep) as a secondary idea; leave the contradictory bearish-sweep names alone.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Downtrend: 90d slope < -0.20%/day, 60d slope < -0.25%/day, accelerating decline
  • Oversold: RSI(14) < 30, price < -15% below EMA20, < -30% below EMA50, Bollinger %B < 0, <20% above 52w low
  • Capitulation: 10d/30d volume > 1.50, ATR ratio > 1.5, ≥7 red days out of 10, ≥2 down gaps, reversal bar
  • Signs of Strength: slope deceleration, higher lows, 1-5 day bounce off the low, bullish RSI divergence
  • Persistence bonus: signal seen ≥10 times in 15d = +20, ≥5 times = +10
Score
Downtrend (max 30) + Oversold (max 25) + Capitulation (max 45) + SOS (max 35) + Persistence (0/10/20). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape confirms the KEEP verdict at a discount to backtest - live win rate is 52.7% versus the backtest 63.3%, but the +3.9% average still beats SPY by 3.8 points, and monthly cohorts trending from January's -12.9% average to June's +5.4% suggest the screener is environment-dependent rather than structurally impaired.

JKS at 100 and ABTC at 92 are the two names worth attention today, both Very strong tier, which is the only live-tracking tier with credible results (68.75% WR, +7.40% average return). SOC, ELTX, and LVWR score 80 to 88 but land in the Strong tier, which tracks at 37% WR and -1.67% average in live data - fade those despite the surface scores. All 50 signals score 65 or above with a median of 71 and only 5 clearing 80, so the batch is tightly clustered with a thin apex. Macro sits at NEUTRAL (51.7), neither a tailwind nor a drag, and does not change the setup materially. Take JKS first at moderate conviction, sizing small given the MARGINAL backtest stamp and the broken Strong tier.

Data confidence HALF SIZE · Sharpe 0.73 Stocks-on-the-Move, walk-forward Sharpe 0.73 (+45.6% vs SPY); trend-quality factor, not a per-trade trigger Full methodology →
🧠 Ranks US equities by annualized exponential-regression slope times R-squared over 90 days, passing only names above their 100-day MA with SPY above its 200-day MA.
Construction
Andreas Clenow 'Stocks on the Move' (Trading Evolved ch.12). Score = annualized exponential-regression slope (90d) × R², filtered on price > MA100 and max recent gap < 15%. Buys only when SPY > MA200 (regime gate). ATR-based sizing (10 bps risk/position).

HUM is the only name worth touching - a perfect 100 score in the Very strong tier, sitting entirely alone at the top of a 17-signal list where the median score is 8 and only one name clears 65. PANW at 64 is the next closest but lands in Watch, not Strong, and the rest of the list (AMAT 26, CSCO 16, UNH 15) are effectively noise. The score distribution is severely top-heavy and thin: one outlier, sixteen stragglers, nothing in between. The macro regime at NEUTRAL (51.7) means the SPY gate is technically open but the environment is not offering any directional push. HUM first, moderate sizing per ATR rules, everything else on the shelf.

Exact criteria & score formula

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Three names score a perfect 100 - GS, PLD, and UNH - all tagged Very strong, but live 90-day tracking shows that tier is the worst performer at 30% win rate and -11.87% avg 20-day return. The batch is top-heavy: 3 of 6 names at the ceiling, with MS (72, Signal), WFC (66, Signal), and C (62, Watch) the only names sitting in lower tiers that have posted positive live returns. Score distribution is bimodal, a hard cluster at 100 and a tail between 62 and 72 with nothing in between. NEUTRAL macro at 51.7 adds no directional tailwind. If forced to choose, C first on tier-adjusted live comps alone, at low conviction, and the three 100-score names stay on the shelf until the tier inversion shows signs of correcting.

Data confidence HALF SIZE backtest_murphy_intermarket doctrine 8-step: 13 buckets MARGINAL_INVERSE Bonferroni-significant (+6 to +14% SPY at 60-120d after intermarket correlation breaks) Full methodology →
🧠 Fires a contrarian LONG on SPY whenever two or more intermarket correlation pairs breach a 2-sigma threshold in seven days, betting on cross-asset mean reversion.
Construction
Counts intermarket correlation breaks (|z| >= 2 sigma over 7d) across major pairs from intermarket_corr_history; >= 2 broken pairs fires a contrarian BUY on SPY, severity scales with the count.

Data confidence HALF SIZE doctrine 8-step: MARGINAL at h=5d (Sharpe 0.5-0.6), REJECT at h>=10d; 60d academic drift not reproduced Full methodology →
🧠 Catches stocks where a positive EPS surprise and gap-up are followed by sustained price continuation, exploiting slow institutional absorption of new earnings information.
Construction
Post-earnings continuation: positive EPS surprise + gap up + holds gap into 1-3 weeks. Score uses surprise magnitude, gap size, post-gap action, and analyst revision activity.
Top picks: $AEHR
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySurpriseConsistencyPost-earn ret
1AEHR ↑78● Signal$76.15 -6.05%1566.7%218.0%
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • POSITIVE direction (EPS beat), surprise ≥ 5.0%
  • Days since earnings in [1, 20]
  • Consistency (beats in the last 4 quarters) ≥ 2
  • Price ≥ $5, 20d avg volume ≥ 500K; final inclusion if score ≥ 45
Score
surprise (≥20 +40, ≥10 +30, ≥5 +20) + consistency (≥4 +25, ≥3 +15, ≥2 +8) + window (≤3d +20, ≤7 +15, ≤14 +10) + drift (>3% +15, >0 +10). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥45. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day verdict says KEEP - win rate 56%, avg +3.5%, beating SPY by 3.1 points across 497 resolved trades - but the June 2026 cohort cuts against it hard: 38% win rate and avg -0.69% on 137 names versus May's 60% win rate and +3.87%, the June number being the key flag that the recent tape is deteriorating.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Five signals, a thin batch. ANGO leads at a perfect 100 score in the Very Strong tier with a PRIMED pattern - the clearest setup in today's run by a wide margin. AEHR follows at 83, also PRIMED and in the Strong tier, making it the only other name with real conviction behind it. BAC, BLK, and ASML fill out the Signal tier between 65 and 75 with BUY or PRIMED tags but no distinguishing edge over the tier floor. Score distribution is top-heavy: ANGO sits apart, the bottom four cluster tightly in the 65-to-75 band. Macro is NEUTRAL at 51.7, which is not a tailwind for drift continuation, so sizing discipline matters. Take ANGO first at high conviction, treat AEHR as a secondary position, and skip the Signal-tier names today.

🧠 Flags large OTM options blocks (>$200K, 1-10 DTE) on quiet names, confirmed via next-day OI delta to isolate genuine new accumulation.
Construction
Large options block trades (>$200K premium, 1-10 DTE, OTM) on quiet stocks. Confirms next-day with OI delta to verify NEW position vs closing. Score weights total premium, vol/OI ratio, OI confirmation.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Stealth institutional accumulation over 60d of OHLCV (optionable universe)
  • Block day: dollar-volume z-score ≥ 1.5σ vs the 60d baseline (excluding the last 5 days)
  • Stealth: low price impact = low |return| / σ_returns
  • Persistence: number of the last 5 days with dollar-volume z > 1.0; up-day bonus
Score
clamp(vol_z/4)×40 + stealth×30 + (persist_5d/5)×20 + (10 if up_day). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90d tape posts a 57% win rate but only +0.11% avg against SPY's +0.97%, and the monthly cohorts at a steady 52% WR confirm the aggregate edge is thin - the key number is the Very strong tier's 85.7% win rate and +10.2% avg on 7 resolved trades, which is where nearly all the alpha concentrates and why tier discipline matters more than the headline count.

What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

BITO at 94 and CWAN at 93 are the only Very strong prints today, and that tier is the only cohort in 90d tracking with a materially positive mean return. EXPD at 88 leads a cluster of Strong names running from 84 to 88, all pointing LONG with no pattern data available. The distribution is top-heavy and compressed, median 79 with all 50 signals clearing 65 and 24 of 50 scoring above 80, so today's batch has an unusually high floor. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 51.7 is not a meaningful tailwind but does not kill the setup either. Start with BITO at highest conviction given the lone score-94 print; CWAN at 93 is a close second.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Polygon universe: ≥ 260d history, avg dollar-volume ≥ $200K, price ≥ $1
  • Entry: daily close crosses above the daily EMA(200)
  • Mandatory weekly filter: weekly close > weekly EMA(50)
  • Window: cross within the last 5 trading days
Score
60 + freshness×30 (= (5−bars since cross)/5) + 10 if weekly filter active, capped at 100. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape confirms a KILL: 42% win rate, -2.9% average versus SPY's +0.2%, a -3.1 point gap; the June 2026 cohort's 66% win rate and +3.2% average look like a one-month regime outlier against May's near-breakeven -0.6% and the full live-track result.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

AAOI and AIG are the two names that earn a first look in today's Very strong batch, though no pattern data exists for any of the 50 signals, stripping the usual quality filter entirely. All 50 signals score exactly 100 with a median of 100, a ceiling-pinned distribution that reads more like a screener quirk than genuine signal concentration. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 51.7 provides no directional tailwind for a long momentum batch. If forced to rank, AAOI earns the first look given its positioning in high-bandwidth infrastructure, but size small and treat this batch with skepticism.

🧠 Flags stocks where short interest exceeds 20% of float, cost-to-borrow is spiking, and price breaks above a key moving average, targeting mechanical covering-driven moves.
Construction
High short interest (>20% float) + cost-to-borrow spike + technical breakout above key MA. Score blends SI %, days-to-cover, CTB, and price momentum.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $3, 20d avg volume > 300K, market cap $200M-$50B
  • Mandatory gate: short interest ≥ 10% of float AND days-to-cover ≥ 3.0
  • Short interest: ≥30% float (+40), ≥20% (+30), ≥15% (+20)
  • Days to cover: ≥8d (+25), ≥5d (+15), ≥3d (+8)
  • Catalyst (not a falling knife): price > SMA50 AND 20d return > +5% (+20)
  • Volume building 10d/30d > 1.3 (+10), RSI(14) 40-70 not extended (+5)
Score
sum of components 0-100. Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥45. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape confirms the screener's edge overall - 61% win rate, +4.7% average, +3.2 points above SPY - but the tier inversion is the key number: the Very strong cohort (n=15) averaged -7%, while the Signal tier (n=123) averaged +7.4%, meaning elevated scores here predict worse, not better, outcomes, and the monthly cohorts reinforce that the edge is regime-sensitive, with April going negative (-0.7% avg, 44% WR) before recovering in May and June.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

BV (score 100, Very strong) and DFH (90, Very strong) lead today's 50-name batch, but the 90-day tier breakdown is a direct warning against chasing the headline scores: the Very strong tier has produced a 20% win rate and an average -7% over 20 days, the worst outcome of any tier in the dataset. ABX and ACHC (both 85, Strong) sit in a cohort that delivered 58% win rate and +3.2% average, a materially better trade. With 47 of 50 signals at 65 or above, the batch is top-heavy rather than broadly distributed, which narrows the field without improving the top end. Macro is NEUTRAL at 51.7, offering no directional tailwind to amplify a squeeze. First preference is ABX at medium conviction; avoid BV and DFH despite the scores.

🧠 Ranks stocks by relative volume (10/30/90-day avg) multiplied by multi-horizon price momentum to surface volume-led breakout candidates from the Russell 1000.
Construction
Reproduces layer #5 of the @kerberos007 thread ('Volume leader screen from RUT-1000'): stocks ranked by relative volume (V/10d, V/30d, V/90d avg) × multi-horizon price momentum (1d/5d/20d/90d). Filters 20d momentum > 0 and price > 5. Score 0-100 = percentile rank of the leader score across the eligible universe.

ACA and TRV headline at score 100 in the Very strong tier, with CHRW and FNB also at 100 and EXPD, FHN, GRMN, OC at 99 - but pattern data is null across every pick, so the RVOL times momentum composite is the entire thesis with nothing structural underneath any name. NEUTRAL macro at 51.7 is a non-event and neither supports nor undermines the setup. Given the KILL verdict on live tracking, this batch earns no high-conviction long exposure; if forced to one name first, TRV at 100 in a defensive sector is the least-bad entry at minimal size.

Data confidence STARTER backtest_dalton_excess 2026-04 (500 tickers 2022-2026): raw excess = coin flip (WR 51%); WITH extreme-location + tail>=1.8xATR filter +3.18% @10d, N=74 only, no block bootstrap Full methodology →
🧠 Flags 1.8x-ATR rejection tails at value-area extremes to identify mean-reversion entries where price location and tail magnitude both confirm excess.
Construction
Market Profile on daily bars (Dalton, Mind Over Markets): rejection-tail bars >= 1.8x ATR formed at value-area extremes (location <= 30% or >= 70%), the only configuration that backtested positive.
Top picks: $RPHS
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySideTail ATRLoc %Kerberos trap
1RPHS ↑90●●● Very strong$8.79 -0.28%LOW1.8126.8%·
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Excess bar detected (wick ≥ 1.8×ATR14) formed in the last 3 bars
  • LONG direction: excess on the LOW side AND trade location ≤ 30%
  • Universe: last_close > 0, max 500 tickers
Score
tail_atr × 50 (tail_atr = wick / ATR14). LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The live 62.5% win rate and +1.74% avg over 20 days outpace the near-flat SPY baseline, which directionally echoes the filtered backtest edge, but 8 resolved trades is less than half the 20-trade floor for a signal, and the June cohort averaging +0.47% trailing May's +3.85% is a soft warning that the edge may be compressing - the key number is n_resolved=8.

RPHS is the sole signal, scoring 90 (Very strong, LONG), but one name does not constitute a batch. The score distribution is degenerate at n_signals=1, median=max=90, so there is no ranking to apply. Take RPHS long at low conviction, size it accordingly, and treat today as thin rather than actionable at full size.

Data confidence STARTER PEAD gap-up + weekly trigger, small N, mechanically sound but too early for a verdict Full methodology →
🧠 Buys post-earnings drift continuation after a weekly red-candle pullback resolves above the prior week's high, within four weeks of the earnings gap-up.
Construction
Weekly red-candle pullback within first 4 weeks post-earnings gap, then weekly close above prior week's high. Identifies high-quality continuation entries on positive earnings reactions.

#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayGapVol ratioBars ago

ABT is the only name in today's batch, scoring 100 in the Very Strong tier, but the pattern field is null, leaving the trigger detail thin. Macro sits at NEUTRAL with a score of 51.7, which neither amplifies nor kills the setup. The more pressing issue is that Very Strong tier names in the 90-day live track are averaging -3.96% over 20 days, worse than the already-poor overall average of -2.6%, making ABT a low-conviction watch rather than an immediate entry.

🧠 Detects institutional flow signals that survive a dual KVO and Force Index negative filter, removing trades near volume-oscillator crosses to reduce false positives.
Construction
SMART_FLOW signals filtered by skipping any trade with KVO_SIGNAL_CROSS or FORCE_INDEX_FLIP active ±3 days. Volume rules act as a negative filter (combo OR-skip).

1KDP ↑50 Watch$30.53 -1.23%BULLISH SWEEPCALL31$227KOPENING250258STRONG22
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Reads SMART_FLOW signals (2-day lookback)
  • Negative filter: SKIP if KVO_SIGNAL_CROSS OR FORCE_INDEX_FLIP volume rule active ±3 trading days
  • T+1 OI Delta filter: SKIP if call_volume(T+1)/call_volume(T) < 0.50
  • Keeps only signals that pass both filters
Score
original SMART_FLOW score, unchanged (no re-scoring). LONG.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

KDP is the only name today, a BULLISH SWEEP at score 50 in the Watch tier, which is the bottom of the actionable range. Score distribution is as thin as it gets: one signal, median 50, nothing at 65 or above, meaning the filter caught most incoming flow as noise today. A NEUTRAL macro regime scoring 51.7 adds no directional conviction to an already marginal setup. The batch is weak, and KDP at score 50 is a low-conviction first look at best - track it, do not size into it.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • FOMO momentum required: 5d perf ≥ +3.0% AND 4-week perf ≥ +8.0%
  • Confirmed breakout: close > 2% above resistance (60d high) + day volume > 1.5× the 20d average (CONFIRMED +20, else WEAK +5)
  • Regime gate: not archived if VIX > 20 (OOS decay -0.49 Sharpe at VIX 20-25)
Score
perf_5d×0.3 + perf_4w×0.3 + perf_13w×0.2 + return_1y×0.2 + breakout_points (+20/+5). Not bounded 0-100 → shown here rescaled (top pick = 100). LONG.

What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Parabolic trend: 90d slope > +0.25%/day, acceleration (30d > 60d > 90d)
  • Over-extension: RSI(14) > 70, price > +15% above EMA20, > +30% above EMA50, Bollinger %B > 1.0
  • Exhaustion: volume divergence (price up, volume down), ATR ratio > 1.5, ≥7 green days out of 10, ≥2 up gaps
  • Signs of Weakness: 1-5 day rollover off the high, bearish RSI divergence
Score
Trend (max 30) + Extension (max 25) + Exhaustion (max 40) + SOW (max 35). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. SHORT.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts

Today's batch offers zero score differentiation: all 50 signals print at 100, median 99, with the ranker unable to separate any name from any other. AMD and MSFT are the headline names in the perfect-100 cohort, with CRWD and PANW forming a cybersecurity cluster, but no pattern data is present for any pick. Given a live KILL verdict, conviction is zero; if forced to act, AMD first for options liquidity only as a defined-risk structure, but do not size this screener.

🧠 Contrarian long setup: weekly RSI(14) below 30 hard gate, confirmed by daily RSI crossover, candlestick reversal, and demand zone scoring.
Construction
Contrarian RSI(14) reversal: HARD GATE weekly RSI(14) < 30 on the last completed week (coded 2026-06-09), then daily RSI oversold/crossover + candlestick reversal + demethod zone scoring.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • HARD GATE: weekly RSI(14) < 30 on the last completed week (fail-closed, added 2026-06-09)
  • RSI oversold: RSI(14) ≤ 30 (+15), ≤ 20 extreme (+10), ≥4 days under 30 (+5)
  • Reversal candlestick (hammer/doji/piercing/engulfing) on T-0 to T-2
  • RSI crossover: RSI back above 30 today or yesterday
  • Demethod zone: swing-low cluster (3% tol) + proximity to EMA20 (<3% +5) or EMA50 (+3)
  • Volume spike ≥2.0× (+5) + VIX %B regime (BTFD_BOOST +15 to DANGER -10)
Score
RSI Oversold (max 30) + Candlestick (max 25) + Crossover (max 20) + Demethod+EMA (max 15) + Volume (max 10) + Regime (-10 to +15), bounded 0-100. Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Live tracking on 496 resolved trades returns 54.2% win rate and +4.15% avg, beating SPY by 3.2 points and earning a KEEP verdict that directly contradicts the doctrine-rejected backtest, but the monthly cohort compression from 78.8% win rate in March down to 52.6% in June is the number that matters, suggesting the edge is thinning as conditions normalize.

🧠 Detects unusual volume spikes, premium deviation, and OI delta on crypto-linked ETFs to front-run directional moves in crypto-exposed equities.
Construction
Tracks unusual flow on crypto-linked ETFs (BITO, IBIT, FBTC, ETHE, GBTC, etc.) - volume vs mean, premium %, OI delta. Score weights flow intensity + spot crypto correlation.
Top picks: $MSTR
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayRSIRS IBIT 20dRegime
1MSTR ↑55 Watch$98.32 +3.66%45.34-22.8%risk_on
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Fixed 12-ticker universe: spot ETFs (IBIT/FBTC/ETHE/BITO) + equity proxies (MSTR/COIN/MARA/RIOT/…)
  • Filters: price > $2, 21d median USD ADV > $5M
  • Uptrend gate: SMA50 > SMA200 AND price > SMA50
  • Pullback: price between -8% and +1% vs SMA20; RSI(14) 35-55; RS vs IBIT 20d > 0
Score
Uptrend (max 35) + Pullback (max 25) + RSI (max 20) + RS (max 10) + Volume (max 10) ± regime, bounded 0-100. Archived if ≥35. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts

Today's batch is a single name: MSTR at score 55, Watch tier, flagged LONG with a clear pattern. One signal sitting at the screener's lowest actionable tier, with nothing above 65, makes this one of the thinnest outputs possible. Score max and median are identical at 55 because there is nothing else, distribution is not just top-light, it is essentially absent. A neutral macro regime at 51.7 adds no tailwind. Pass on MSTR entirely - Watch-tier output from a backtest-rejected, 90-day kill screener offers no edge, and a single low-score name in a thin tape is not a reason to move.

HACK (69, Signal) is the only name approaching meaningful score, and IBB (66, Signal) is the runner-up, but neither carries a pattern tag, so you are buying direction without a technical setup to anchor entry. VNQ (65, Signal) is the odd inclusion given it is real estate rather than an emerging technology theme, which raises questions about ETF overlap noise in the construction. The distribution is compressed and weak: 50 signals, max 69, median 52, zero above 80, only four above 65. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 51.7 offers no thematic tailwind today. If forced to act, HACK first at low conviction - the score ceiling does not support sizing up.

What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. 🧠 AI read · today's batch

Eight names score 100 at Very strong with LONG direction, but all pattern fields are null, stripping the batch of structural context beyond raw score. BL and CELH are the cleanest single-stock calls at maximum score; SRTY, a 3x inverse Russell 2000 ETF, appearing as a LONG at 100 is the most anomalous entry and deserves a flag before acting. Distribution is broad but high: 50 signals, median 70, 41 of 50 above 65 and 12 above 80, with the top cluster tightly packed at a perfect 100. Macro is NEUTRAL at 51.7, offering no regime tailwind, making this a pick-on-merit situation rather than a macro-aligned setup. BL first at moderate conviction; verify SRTY's instrument type before touching it.

🧠 Tracks aggregate hedge fund conviction in individual names via 13F new/increased position scoring across 20 top-tier funds.
Construction
Quarterly SEC 13F-HR filings from 20 top hedge funds via EDGAR. Whale Score: +20/NEW (cap 60), +10/INCREASED (cap 30), -5/CLOSED, -3/DECREASED. Stale 60-90 days by nature (quarterly).

🧠 AI read · today's batch

The score distribution is fully saturated - all 50 signals sit at 100, median 100, so the screener offers zero internal ranking today and the output is a consensus long list, not differentiated alpha. MKSI and LITE are the least obvious names in the batch, and whale clustering in smaller-float names carries more informational weight than the same score on AAPL or META, where any large fund adding shares is unremarkable. SPOT is worth separating from the mega-cap noise as a non-consensus pick where a score of 100 signals genuine conviction. The NEUTRAL macro regime at 51.7 provides no tailwind for 13F momentum plays, which require sustained trend to work given their structural delay. First preference is MKSI at low conviction, the smaller float means the whale signal is harder to dismiss as passive drift, but size tight given the 60-90 day filing lag baked into every name on this list.

FINS and JEF both score 100, the cleanest names by the screener's own ranking formula, with COE and STDN close behind at 98. Pattern data is null across all eight top picks, so score and dollar-weighted seniority are the only available differentiators. The score distribution is maximally compressed: all 47 signals clear 80 and the median sits at 94, meaning the screener is not separating the batch at all today - every name looks alike by these metrics. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 51.7 offers no directional tailwind, so follow-through depends on company-specific catalysts not visible in this data. Start with FINS at moderate conviction, but the screen alone gives thin signal - independent diligence is required before sizing.

Data confidence Starter · untested · no rigorous backtest yet, queued for the untested-screeners battery. Methodology →
🧠 Ranks non-financial US equities by EV/EBIT to surface the cheapest operating businesses, exploiting mean reversion in depressed enterprise valuations.
Construction
Tobias Carlisle's Acquirer's Multiple = Enterprise Value / operating earnings (EBIT). Universe is market cap > $200M, excludes Financial Services and Utilities (EV distorted), drops negative operating earnings. Ranks the universe by multiple ascending and surfaces the cheapest names (score 100 = cheapest of the day).
Top picks: $BZ $WRD $KROS

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $200M, excludes Financial Services & Utilities (EV distorted)
  • Acquirer's Multiple = Enterprise Value / operating earnings (EBIT, operating income)
  • Operating earnings from Polygon financials (operating_income / EBIT); EV from yfinance else proxy market cap + non-current liabilities
  • Drops negative operating earnings; ranks the universe by multiple ascending (cheapest first)
Score
score = inverse rank scaled 0-100 (cheapest of the day = 100). LONG (deep value selection).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day resolved tape (57% win rate, +5.1% average, beating SPY by 4.5 points, verdict KEEP) gives a constructive read, but the only monthly cohort - June 2026 at 20% win rate and flat average return - is a direct contradiction, and with just 21 resolved trades the tape cannot yet dismiss it.

BZ leads today at a score of 100 in the Very strong tier, followed by WRD at 97 and KROS at 93, both also Very strong but with no pattern confirmation on any name in the batch. 🧠 Flags stocks where 3 or more insiders buy within 30 days, scoring cluster size, dollar value, rank, and recency to surface coordinated conviction.
Construction
SEC Form 4 cluster buys: 3+ insiders buying within 30 days, with C-suite or director rank weighted higher. Score uses cluster size, total $ value, insider rank, and recency.
Top picks: $CTM $FNWD $GLOO
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayBuysCXOClusterAvg value
1CTM ↑60 Watch$0.61 -3.07%4✓✓$1K

4LILA ↑60 Watch$7.28 -0.89%4✓✓$1175K
5SWZ ↑60 Watch$5.93 +0.04%3✓✓$30K
6TSM ↑60 Watch$402.19 +0.96%33✓✓$8K

9KMX ↑55 Watch$55.91 -2.48%3·✓$213K
10COE ↑50 Watch$16.31 -1.27%6✓·$12471K
11PRQR ↑50 Watch$1.74 +2.06%3✓·$244K
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • At least 1 insider buy on the ticker
  • Cluster if ≥ 3 unique insiders within a 14-day window
  • C-level detected if title is CEO/CFO/COO/CTO/President/Chairman
  • Average transaction-size tiers: ≥$200K or ≥$100K
Score
buy base (≥2 +15, 1 +5) + cluster +25 + C-level +20 + avg (≥200K +15, ≥100K +8), cap 60. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live tape supports keeping this screener active - 62% win rate and a +5.6% avg 20-day return against SPY's -0.38% is a genuine edge - but April's cohort avg of +18.9% was inflated by a single +539% outlier and no formal backtest has ever been run, so the 62% WR is the only number worth anchoring to until the untested-screeners battery runs.

Data confidence No backtest stats yet (N < 30 or not run) · starter size, discretionary read. Methodology & all-screeners comparison →
🧠 Identifies stocks lagging peers within already-moving sectors, scoring on sector relative strength multiplied by individual stock underperformance to find catch-up candidates.
Construction
Within LATE_CHASE / RIDING sectors, identifies the lagging individual stocks that haven't yet caught the sector move. Score = sector relative strength × stock under-performance.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is weak and should be treated as a watchlist placeholder, not an actionable setup. All 31 signals score exactly 50, the maximum and median are identical, and zero names clear 65, meaning the scoring formula is returning a degenerate flat distribution with no differentiation. Pattern data is null across every pick, so the only structure visible is sector membership: CCI and SBAC are tower REITs lagging a potential real-estate sector bid, and DLR is a data-center REIT with similar logic, making that infrastructure-REIT cluster the most coherent thematic thread in the batch. Macro regime sits at NEUTRAL with a 51.7 score, which neither supports nor kills a catch-up trade but does nothing to rescue a scoring output this underpowered. If forced to prefer one name first, CCI is the most liquid of the infrastructure trio and the easiest to exit, but conviction is low given the flat score distribution.

🧠 Ranks macro ETFs via Antonacci dual momentum, requiring both positive 12-month absolute return and top relative rank among peers to signal long.
Construction
Antonacci dual momentum on a macro ETF set: absolute (12m return > 0) AND relative (top rank vs peers). Long the leaders.

IWM tops the batch at 90 (Very strong), but live tracking shows that tier carries a 0% win rate and a -5.2% average 20-day return, making the top label the worst live performer despite the highest score. Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=0 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Detects highest-RS tickers inside bullish sectors, scoring on 20-day RS vs SPY, sector breadth, and cluster rank to capture rotation momentum.
Construction
Top relative-strength tickers within bullish sectors. Score = relative strength vs SPY (20d) + sector breadth + cluster ranking.
Top picks: $ABX $ACA $ACAD
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySectorRet 1mRet 3m
1ABX ↑80●● Strong$10.35 -0.10%Financials18.7%30.6%
2ACA ↑68● Signal$144.71 -0.21%Industrials12.2%27.2%
3ACAD ↑64 Watch$25.27 -1.29%Healthcare18.9%13.9%
4ABVX ↑58 Watch$134.04 -1.91%Healthcare40.6%7.9%
5ACCO ↑57 Watch$4.01 -1.59%Industrials-2.5%25.5%
6AAMI ↑55 Watch$83.08 +0.34%Financials0.8%21.8%
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $300M
  • Step 1: bullish sectors = sector ETF (XLK/XLV/…) with 20d return > 0
  • Step 2: score the stocks in each bullish sector, top 10/sector
Score
Momentum 0-100 = 1M Ret (30%) + 3M Ret (30%) + RS vs SPY 3M (20%) + Volume Trend (20%), normalized components. ≥70 strong. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 No formal backtest exists yet at N=0, so the 90-day live tape is the only evidence base, and it holds up: 57% win rate and +4.4% average versus SPY's -1.7% delivers +6.1 points of alpha, a figure the May cohort's 55.7% win rate across 176 names broadly confirms, though the 1,149% best-case outlier in May inflates that cohort's +10.8% mean and warrants skepticism about the average as a sizing guide.

A NEUTRAL macro regime at 51.7 provides no sector momentum tailwind, and that mutes conviction on the whole batch. ACA is the preferred first look at moderate conviction; ABX earns only small size until the Strong-tier live track record closes the gap with Signal.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=0 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 TRAP_SHORT detects fake breakouts above the value area high that close back inside, trapping longs and generating a short signal.
Construction
Fake breakout above VAH followed by a close back inside the value area = bull trap, longs trapped, SHORT signal.
Top picks: $ALV $AMCX $ACCO
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayTrap typeStopTarget POC
1ALV ↓95●●● Very strong$117.89 -1.97%BULL_TRAP122.87116.23
2AMCX ↓75● Signal$10.67 +1.72%BULL_TRAP11.209.90
3ACCO ↓65● Signal$4.01 -1.59%BULL_TRAP4.273.92
4ALLY ↓65● Signal$45.55 -0.21%BULL_TRAP47.0742.55
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • BULL_TRAP (SHORT): close[N-1] > VAH then close[N] < VAH (failed bullish breakout rejected)
  • fake_dist and reclaim_dist in [0.2, 2.0] ATR; total pct_move ≤ 12%
  • score ≥ 55 required
Score
50 base + volume_ratio (>1.5 +15) + fake_dist (>1.0 +10) + reclaim_dist (>0.5 +10) + rejection_wick (>0.5 +10) + cleanness (+5), bounded 0-100. SHORT.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 No backtest exists (N=0 resolved trades), so the 90-day live data is the only reference: 54% WR and a -0.95% average versus SPY at -0.38%, a -0.6 pt gap the June cohort extends with average losses widening to -1.6%, leaving the KILL verdict intact.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=25 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags tickers where the IV30 minus HV20 spread exceeds 1.5 standard deviations on a 90-day baseline, surfacing inflated fear premium for short-vol selling.
Construction
IV30 − HV20 spread spike on individual tickers (z-score > 1.5σ on 90d). Detects unusual fear premium - candidates for premium selling.

#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30HV30RatioZone

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • ATM IV30 / realized HV30 ratio (price ≥ $10, ≥35 days of closes)
  • Zones: ≥2.0 EXTREME_FEAR, ≥1.5 FEAR, ≥1.2 ELEVATED, 1.0-1.2 NORMAL, <1.0 COMPLACENT
  • Archives only FEAR and EXTREME_FEAR
Score
min(100, ratio × 40) where ratio = IV30/HV30. NEUTRAL.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=1 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags pre-earnings names where straddle IV trades below Yang-Zhang HV20 expected move, identifying cheap vol before a catalyst.
Construction
Pre-earnings (3-14d to event) with straddle IV < expected move from Yang-Zhang HV20. Identifies when implied vol underprices realized expectation.
Top picks: $XOM $ROKU
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySectionDTEExpectedPlay
1XOM ↑75● Signal$148.75 +0.95%PRE_IV_RAMP11—BUY CALL 30-45 DTE
2ROKU ↑50 Watch$144.53 +0.07%PRE_IV_RAMP10—BUY CALL 30-45 DTE
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Market cap ≥ $1B
  • PRE_IV_RAMP: earnings DTE 7-14d AND IV Rank < 50 (premium not yet expensive)
  • POST_PEAD: days_since earnings 0-5d
  • REVERSAL: earnings ≤ 2d AND pre-earnings drift ≥ 3%
Score
by section: PRE_IV_RAMP (DTE + IV rank + consistency + mcap, max 100), POST_PEAD (surprise + consistency + days + drift, max 100), REVERSAL (|drift| + DTE, max 60).
🧠 AI read · today's batch

KO at 75 is the best read here, a Signal-tier name sitting just below the 80 threshold where conviction would be cleaner. CVX scores 65 and also holds Signal status, though no pattern data is attached to either name, which limits the setup thesis to the raw IV gap alone. V at 55 is a Watch only and does not warrant action. Three names total, no score above 80, median 65 - this is a thin, compressed batch with no standout setup. In a NEUTRAL macro regime (51.7), the edge is modest at best; KO is the first look at a small position, low conviction.

Construction
Detects retail-driven euphoria: retail call volume spike, Reddit mention surge, parabolic price, no earnings catalyst. Score combines social attention z-score + call/put + price stretch.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • call_volume / call_open_interest ratio (euphoria contrarian)
  • Tiers: ≥1.00 FOMO, ≥0.75 EUPHORIA, ≥0.50 GREED (scan threshold 0.50)
  • Filters: price > $10, options volume > 1000, total OI > 4000
Score
clamp(0,100, call_vol_oi_ratio × 100). SHORT (fade the retail crowd).

🧠 Detects volatility mispricing via IV rank, IV30/HV20 ratio, skew, term structure, and Kerberos-style options flow in a composite experimental score.
Construction
Research-lab composite: IV rank + IV30/HV20 ratio + skew + term structure + Kerberos-style flow. Experimental scoring R1.

#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30IV rankHV20Rule
1UVXY ↓85●● Strong$24.62 -3.22%81%861%RULE_1
2VXX ↓85●● Strong$22.03 -2.00%55%443%RULE_1
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Vol-decay basket (UVXY, VXX, SVXY, SDS, QID, SQQQ, FAZ, TZA)
  • Rule 1 (3 conditions required): IV30 < 100% AND IV Rank < 10 AND VIX3M/VIX > 1.0 (contango)
  • 2/3 conditions → inactive (score 55)
Score
if 3/3 active: min(100, 85 + max(0, 3−iv_rank)). NEUTRAL.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=3 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 BEAR_SKEW flags stocks where put IV exceeds call IV above the 90th percentile, treating extreme skew as a contrarian long trigger.
Construction
Bearish IV skew bias: put IV >> call IV at extreme percentiles (>90th). Detects unusual put demand.
Top picks: $AVGO
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySkew ptsIV30IV rankVerdict
1AVGO ↑100●●● Very strong$379.49 +2.34%16.7468.7%97BEAR_SKEW_EXTREME
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • 25Δ skew (30 DTE target) > +5 vol points (puts overpriced)
  • Earnings in 0-7 days (catalyst near)
  • IV Rank > 60 (high premium = fear)
  • Verdict: skew>10pts + IVRank>80 EXTREME, skew>7pts HIGH, else MILD
Score
clamp(50,100, 50 + skew_25d_pts × 3). LONG (BTFD contrarian: fear overpriced).

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=10 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Fades abnormal put/call skew spikes vs 90-day baseline as contrarian longs, edge assumes crowded hedging demand reverses when underlying price stays calm.
Construction
25-delta skew (put IV − call IV) spike vs 90d baseline. Bearish skew = hedging demand spike, contrarian read when stock is calm.
Top picks: $MSTR
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30IV rankSkew 25dPlaybook
1MSTR ↑57 Watch$98.32 +3.66%66%960.03BUY PREMIUM (IV cheap)
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Cross-section scan of the watchlist (large caps + ETFs)
  • Skew_Z = (skew_25d − basket median) / basket std
  • IV_Rich_Z = (IV_HV_spread − basket median) / basket std
  • Composite = IV_Rich_Z×1.0 + Skew_Z×0.5; archived if |Composite| ≥ 1.5
Score
int(|Composite| × 20). SHORT vol if Composite>0 (IV rich), LONG vol if <0.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Live tracking confirms the screener is productive, 54% WR and +6.2% mean 20-day return beating SPY by 4.9 points, but the June cohort cracked to -3.1% average after May's +10.1%, and the tier structure inverts badly, Very Strong and Strong signals averaging -5.6% and -6.2% while Watch signals average +13.0%, all of which contradicts the premise that higher skew intensity is better and the backtest remains unvalidated at N=10.

Refresh
EOD
Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=0 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Daily close outside VAL/VAH followed by a next-bar reclaim traps the initiating side and generates a mean-reversion entry signal.
Construction
Kerberos Apr-2020 thesis adapted to daily bars: a close below VAL (fake breakdown) followed by a reclaim next day = bear trap, shorts trapped, LONG signal. Mirror logic above VAH for bull traps.
Top picks: $APA
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayTrap typeStopTarget POCATR
1APA ↑70● Signal$35.30 +0.23%BEAR_TRAP34.0836.831.23
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • BEAR_TRAP (LONG): close[N-1] < VAL then close[N] > VAL (failed bearish breakout reclaimed)
  • fake_dist and reclaim_dist in [0.2, 2.0] ATR; total pct_move ≤ 12%
  • score ≥ 55 required
Score
50 base + volume_ratio (>1.5 +15) + fake_dist (>1.0 +10) + reclaim_dist (>0.5 +10) + rejection_wick (>0.5 +10) + cleanness (+5), bounded 0-100. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 With no formal backtest to compare against (N=0, not yet validated), the 90-day live tape stands alone: 67% WR and a +5.7% avg that beats SPY by 5.6 points, confirmed directionally by both monthly cohorts (May 76.9%, June 64.4%), with the fade into June the one number to watch as trade count scales.

Altman

ALTMANEOD⚠ Dormant · 2026-05-23 (37d)
Live: base version (no variants documented)
Today's best
Watch
0 signals ≥65 · 0 shown
Data confidence
STARTER · untested
no backtest stats yet
Refresh
EOD
Data confidence No backtest stats yet (N < 30 or not run) · starter size, discretionary read. Methodology & all-screeners comparison →
🧠 Screens equities using Altman Z-Score components to flag financial distress; low scores historically precede credit stress or forced deleveraging events.
Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $300M, excludes Financial Services & Utilities (model not applicable)
  • Altman Z = 1.2×(WC/TA) + 1.4×(RE/TA) + 3.3×(EBIT/TA) + 0.6×(MktCap/TL) + 1.0×(Rev/TA)
  • Financials from Polygon (else yfinance)
  • Output filter: keeps only Z < 2.5 (grey + distress)
Score
score = the Z-Score itself. Zones: Z>2.99 SAFE, 1.81-2.99 GREY, <1.81 DISTRESS, <1.0 critical. LONG (risk filter).

Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: price > $10 (shortable)
  • Analyst gate: recommendationMean ≥ 3.0 (Hold or worse)
  • Fundamental gate: EVA Proxy Score ≤ 40 (low quality)
  • EVA Proxy 0-100 = 5 blocks of 20 pts: Profitability, Growth, Balance Sheet, Cash Flow, Valuation
Score
score = EVA Proxy (0-100, lower = worse). Top 50 sorted ascending (worst first). SHORT.

Data confidence No backtest stats yet (N < 30 or not run) · starter size, discretionary read. Methodology & all-screeners comparison →
🧠 VSA flags institutional accumulation or distribution by comparing price spread and close position against volume, seeking supply/demand imbalances before directional moves.
Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $3, 20d avg volume > 200K, market cap > $50M
  • Per-bar metrics (last 5): relative volume vs SMA20, normalized spread, close position within the range
  • Bullish patterns: No Supply, Testing, Stopping Volume, Absorption, Falling Pressure, Failed Down Move
  • Bearish patterns: No Demand, Up-Thrust, Buying Climax, Effort To Rise Failed
  • 20-bar accumulation/distribution background + Relative Strength vs SPY 20d
Score
best-pattern priority (No Supply 3 → Buying Climax 6) + background alignment (+2/-1) + RS vs SPY alignment (+1). Top 40. LONG or SHORT depending on pattern.

Not included on this page (by design)
These Google-Sheet tabs are not per-ticker screeners, so they have no card here: ETF_RANKING (asset-class ranking), CPR_RANK (Camarilla pivot assist), SECTOR_STRENGTH / SECTOR_BREADTH / SECTOR_ROTATION (sector-level gauges, see the macro cockpit), SKEW_VELOCITY / VOLATILITY_DASHBOARD / VVIX market panel (market-level vol gauges, see the cockpit), STRUCTURAL_FLOWS (OpEx / quarter-end calendar), QQQ_PAIRS / PAIR_ZSCORE (pair-trading lab, REJECT OOS), NEWS_SENTIMENT (newsletter prep data), MULTI_STRATEGY / META_SCREENER (aggregators, superseded by Best Trades), plus journal / portfolio / content tabs. Macro composites (BTFD index, COT, Kerberos PCR) live on the cockpit and macro pages.
Generated by build_screeners_deep.py · sources: signal_archive, whale_13f, openinsider, hrhr_ranked, options_flow_shortlist, emergence_history, sector_acceleration/laggards
Data confidence scale = a discretionary sizing guide (full / half / starter), never a permission verdict · stats from screener_validity + block-bootstrap v12
Sparklines = ~6 months daily close · AI reads regenerate at the EOD build, page refreshes hourly during US market hours
